records.ety: [OE. laund, launde, F. lande heath, moor; of Celtic origin; cf. W. llan an open, clear place, llawnt a smooth rising hill, lawn, Armor. lann or lan territory, country, lann a prickly plant, pl. lannou heath, moor.]

defs: Ground (generally in front of or around a house) covered with grass kept closely mown.

records.ety: [Earlier laune lynen, i. e., lawn linen; prob. from the town Laon in France.]

defs: A very fine linen (or sometimes cotton) fabric with a rather open texture. Lawn is used for the sleeves of a bishop's official dress in the English Church, and, figuratively, stands for the office itself.
quotes: A saint in crape is twice a saint in lawn.
